meaning: n.(靠近意大利一边的危险岩石)锡拉岩礁
examples: He gives up the missing piece, and Scylla boots up.
Self asks Trishanne if there were other buyers for Scylla.
Christina takes Scylla from the briefcase Mahone gave her.
It seems that there are in fact two bills--sort of a legislative Scylla and Charybdis--calling for the creation of a Federal CIO.
In such a situation, you must make the safest choice: neither the Scylla of nationalization nor the Charybdis of a keep-your-fingers-crossed free market ideology.
